What this document covers

Overview

This hand-off covers the before vs after comparison of three page types based on a direct audit of the existing HTML files and the revamped HTML files. Each section shows the existing code issue (left panel, amber) against the revamped implementation (right panel, navy) with cropped page screenshots and real code evidence extracted by Python from the actual HTML files.

Each page includes a SEO · AEO · GEO signal card summary explaining why each change matters across organic ranking, answer engine eligibility, and LLM citation surfaces.
⚠️
Critical — Back-port mega menu before homepage goes liveThe mega menu (.mega / .pni / .pni-btn pattern with all sport sub-links) exists in trophies-listing.html but is completely absent from index.html. This is the highest-priority task — without it Google cannot discover 60+ sport sub-pages from the homepage nav.
PageCritical Issues Found in CodeSchema AddedImages FixedPriority
HomepageNo mega menu, 8× ShowTab()+return false, 6-Q FAQ only, 51 missing altWebSite (SearchAction) + Organization + ItemList + FAQPage51 → 0P1
Product ListingSub-category URL scope, no hero H1, missing CollectionPage/FAQPage/AggregateRatingCollectionPage + Organization/AggregateRating + FAQPage25 → 0P1
Product DetailDual H1 ("FREE LOGO CENTRE"), title trailing pipe, zero product schema, no cross-sellProduct + Offer + AggregateRating + FAQPage26 → 0P1
Page 1 of 3 — Homepage
Page 1 of 3

Homepage — alphatrophies.com.au

The homepage had 52 onclick elements (8 category tab triggers with return false; each, plus additional JS-driven interactions), no mega menu structure in the nav HTML, only a 6-question FAQPage, and 51 images with missing or empty alt text. Schema was WebSite + LocalBusiness + FAQPage — missing Organization (3 Place objects), ItemList with Offer prices, and WebSite SearchAction. The revamp adds four schema types, removes all onclick intercepts from category navigation, and replaces the image-text banner with a full DOM-text hero with stat bar.
52
onclick elements
in existing HTML
0
mega menu dropdowns
in nav HTML
6
FAQPage questions
(need 10)
51
images missing alt
(of 80 total)
SEO

Crawl Equity & Ranking Signals

  • Removing return false; from 8 category onclick triggers → Google confidently follows all category links as standard <a href> navigation
  • Mega menu back-port exposes 60+ sport sub-pages as crawlable links from root — the single biggest internal PageRank lever on the site
  • Hero H2 in DOM ("New in: corporate awards for 2025") replaces non-semantic div text — indexed keyword signal for seasonal commercial queries
  • 4 schema types vs 2: WebSite (SearchAction), Organization (3×Place, 1985, 4.9/169), ItemList (4 products + Offer prices), FAQPage (10 Q&As) → SERP searchbox, star rating, product cards, FAQ snippets
  • Alt text fixed on 51 images → image search equity + Core Web Vitals alt compliance
AEO

AI Overview & Answer Eligibility

  • 10 FAQPage Q&As with price anchors ("from $7.35"), timeframes ("1-2 business days") and location data → each is a Google AI Overview answer candidate
  • Organization schema with foundingDate (1985) + aggregateRating (4.9/169) → trust signals AI engines display alongside answers
  • ItemList with Offer prices → product carousel in AI Overviews for "buy trophies Australia" commercial queries
  • WebSite SearchAction schema → sitelinks searchbox in SERP for brand queries — reduces zero-click bounce for navigational searches
  • Specific attributable answers (not vague marketing copy) satisfy the E-E-A-T bar AI engines apply before citing a page
GEO

LLM Citation Readiness

  • llm.txt footer link → signals GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot that content is crawl-permitted for AI training and citation
  • Stat bar copy in DOM ("40+ years", "10,000+ products", "4.9 stars from 169 reviews") → LLMs cite these specific numbers as verifiable facts
  • 3 store addresses in Organization JSON-LD (Melbourne VIC / Adelaide SA / Sydney NSW) → LLMs surface Alpha Trophies for location-based prompts
  • Occasion-based content clusters (Sports clubs / Schools / Corporates / Events) → aligns with LLM topic classification of trophy buyer intent
  • Verbatim, dated review quotes in page DOM → LLMs weight these as E-E-A-T signals for brand trust assessment
1
Navigation

Nav Header & Mega Menu

The existing nav has 451 links in a single <nav class="navbar"> but zero .dropdown-menu elements. All 8 category links fire ShowTab() and return false — intercepting navigation even though href attributes are present. The revamped nav uses clean <a> tags with a full position:fixed mega menu panel — which must be back-ported from trophies-listing.html into index.html.

BEFORE — ShowTab() onclick intercepts all category links
Existing code — all 8 category links: 
<!-- Every category nav link fires ShowTab() + return false -->
<a href="https://alphatrophies.com.au/products/trophies"
   onclick="ShowTab('alpha-3', this);return false;">
  Trophies
</a>
<!-- Same pattern for: Trophy Cups, Medals, Corporate Awards,
     School Awards, Rosettes, Gifts, Perpetual (8 total)
     return false blocks href; signals JS widget not a link -->
Python audit confirmed: 0 .dropdown-menu, 0 .dropdown LIs.
Mega menu is completely absent from index.html.
AFTER — Clean <a> tags + mega menu (back-port required)
Revamped — clean nav links, no onclick intercepts: 
<a class="ni on"
   href="https://alphatrophies.com.au/products/trophies">
  Trophies
</a>
<!-- No onclick, no return false — Google follows directly -->
⚠️
Action required — back-port from trophies-listing.htmlCopy the .mega, .pni, .pni-btn component and all sport sub-links HTML into index.html. The homepage nav is flat without this.
/* CSS to copy into index.html */
.mega { position:fixed; top:107px;
  background:var(--navy);
  border-top:2px solid var(--gold);
  opacity:0; visibility:hidden; }
.mega.on { opacity:1; visibility:visible; }
2
Hero Section

Hero Banner — DOM Text vs Div Text

The existing banner headline is in a <div class="text"> with no H tag semantic weight. The actual H1 appears mid-page below the fold. The revamp moves the primary message into an H2 within the hero section, uses CSS background-image for the visual, and adds a stat bar with all numbers as text in DOM.

BEFORE — No H tag in banner, H1 below the fold
Existing structure (confirmed in code): 
<!-- Banner text — no H tag, just a div -->
<div class="text">
  Australia's only truly National Trophy Supplier
</div>

<!-- Actual H1 — mid-page, below the fold -->
<h1>Australia's Largest Trophies
  &amp; Awards Shop Online</h1>

<!-- Meta description (existing) — no price anchor -->
"Discover premium trophies &amp; medals for schools,
colleges &amp; businesses at Alpha Trophies."
AFTER — Hero H2 in DOM, stat bar, 3-slide carousel
Revamped — hero H2 and stat bar in DOM: 
<h2 class="s-h">New in: corporate
  <em>awards for 2025.</em></h2>
<p class="s-p">Crystal, glass and acrylic awards...</p>

<!-- Stat bar (all text in DOM, not image) -->
<div class="sb-n">40+</div><div class="sb-l">Years</div>
<div class="sb-n">10,000+</div><div class="sb-l">Products</div>
<div class="sb-n">$9.90</div><div class="sb-l">Flat-rate</div>
3
Category Architecture

Browse By Category — onclick → crawlable anchors

16 onclick=ShowTab() elements in the category section use return false. The 401 links inside the category parent div are sub-category links managed by JS tab visibility — they exist in HTML but only one set is visible at a time. The revamp replaces the tab triggers with real <button> panels containing crawlable product cards and sport sub-links as anchor tags.

BEFORE — 16 onclick triggers, return false, JS-toggled links
Category section audit results: 
// Python audit on existing-homepage.html:
onclick in category section: 16
links in same parent div:    401  // JS-toggled, not all crawlable
category images:             8   // all missing/empty alt
All 8 tab triggers confirmed: 
<a href=".../products/medals"
   onclick="ShowTab('alpha-5', this);return false;">
  Medals</a>
<a href=".../products/corporate-awards"
   onclick="ShowTab('alpha-7', this);return false;">
  Corporate Awards</a>
<!-- 6 more, all identical pattern -->
AFTER — Tab panels with real <a href> product and sport links
Revamped — button tabs + real anchor sub-sport links: 
<!-- Tab buttons (no return false) -->
<button class="ct on"
  onclick="showCat('sport',this)">
  Sport Trophies</button>

<!-- Inside panel — real crawlable sport links -->
<a class="sp"
   href="/products/trophies?sport=football">
  Football</a>

<!-- Product card with correct alt text -->
<a href="/product/netball-econo-medal">
  <img
    alt="Explore Netball Econo Medal starting from $6.50"
    title="Buy Best Netball Medals Online from Alpha Trophies from $6.50"
  >
</a>
4
Schema & FAQ

FAQPage — 6 generic questions → 10 with full schema stack

Existing FAQPage has 6 generic questions with no price anchors or location specificity. Schema stack: WebSite + LocalBusiness + FAQPage. Revamp upgrades to 4 types: WebSite (SearchAction), Organization (3 Place objects, foundingDate 1985, aggregateRating 4.9/169), ItemList (4 products with Offer prices), and FAQPage (10 Q&As with specific commercial-intent answers).

BEFORE — 6 questions, LocalBusiness, no SearchAction or ItemList
Existing FAQPage (6 questions confirmed): 
{"@type":"FAQPage","mainEntity":[
  {"name":"How long does trophy engraving take?"},
  {"name":"Do you ship trophies Australia wide?"},
  {"name":"Is there a minimum order quantity?"},
  {"name":"Can I customise with my club logo?"},
  {"name":"What types of trophies do you sell?"},
  {"name":"Do you have physical stores?"}
// 6 total — generic, no price anchors, no locations
]}
Existing schema types: WebSite · LocalBusiness · FAQPage
Missing: Organization (3 Place), ItemList, SearchAction
AFTER — 10 Q&As, 4-type schema stack, 2-col accordion
Revamped — 4-type schema stack: 
[
  {"@type":"WebSite",
    "potentialAction":{"@type":"SearchAction"}},
  {"@type":"Organization",
    "foundingDate":"1985",
    "aggregateRating":{
      "ratingValue":"4.9",
      "reviewCount":"169"},
    "location":[Melbourne, Adelaide, Sydney]},
  {"@type":"ItemList",
    // 4 products with Offer prices (AUD)
    "itemListElement":[...]},
  {"@type":"FAQPage",
    // 10 Q&As with price anchors + location data
    "mainEntity":[{
      "name":"What trophies are best for a sports club?",
      "acceptedAnswer":{"text":
        "Resin sport trophies from $18..."}}]}
]
Page 2 of 3 — Product Listing
Page 2 of 3

Product Listing — /products/trophies

The uploaded listing file is a baseball sub-category page (/products/trophies/b/baseball) — not the root /products/trophies URL. Title: "Shop Baseball Trophies Online | Free engraving". H1: "Baseball Trophies". The root listing URL may be entirely under-optimised. Schema had only BreadcrumbList + ItemList — missing CollectionPage, FAQPage and AggregateRating. 32 onclick elements are GA4 tracking events (not a crawl issue — href is present) but all 16 product images have empty alt="". The revamped version targets the root listing with a full-width hero H1, stat strip, mega menu and structured below-fold content hub.

SEO

Head-Term Ranking & SERP Features

  • H1 "Buy Trophies Online in Australia" on the root /products/trophies URL → exact-match for primary commercial head term (existing H1 was "Baseball Trophies" on a sub-URL)
  • CollectionPage JSON-LD → breadcrumb rich result → multi-level SERP path visible to users before they click
  • Organization/AggregateRating (4.9/169) in schema → star rating in SERP snippet → estimated 15-30% CTR uplift on the highest-traffic listing URL
  • FAQPage (10 Q&As) → expandable FAQ rows below SERP snippet → dominates more search result real estate
  • Mega menu from nav distributes PageRank to 60+ sport sub-pages from this high-equity URL
AEO

Rich Results & AI Overview Slots

  • 10 FAQPage Q&As with specific answers ("from $7.35", "1-2 business days", "no minimum order") → each is a Google AI Overview candidate for informational trophy queries
  • Trophy type comparison HTML <table> (Resin vs Metal Cup vs Medal vs Glass) → Google can surface this as a tabular comparison answer
  • "How ordering works" 4-step process → targets "how to order trophies Australia" — formatted for step-result rich snippets
  • ItemList schema with Offer prices (16 products) → product carousel in AI Overviews for "buy trophies" commercial queries
  • AggregateRating (4.9/169) → star snippet in SERP → primary trust signal for purchase-intent clicks
GEO

LLM Citation & Brand Surface Area

  • Stat strip ("2,400+ products", "60+ sports") in DOM → LLMs cite specific numbers as verifiable facts about Alpha Trophies
  • Review quotes (verbatim, attributed, dated) → LLMs weight these as E-E-A-T signals for brand quality
  • Trophy comparison table content (Resin vs Metal) → LLMs use structured comparisons to answer "what is the best trophy for a sports club?" and cite the source
  • Location data (3 store addresses in schema) → LLMs surface Alpha Trophies for "trophy shop near me" location prompts
  • llm.txt footer link → crawl-permission signal for AI indexers on the highest-traffic product page
1
Navigation

Mega Menu Implementation

The listing page already implements the full mega menu that the homepage is missing. This is the reference implementation for the back-port. All nav links are clean <a> tags. The BreadcrumbList schema is correctly present.

BEFORE — Flat nav, 0 dropdown elements, ShowTab() drives all links
Confirmed — no .dropdown-menu in existing nav: 
// Python audit result on existing-product-listing.html:
dropdown LIs:    0
dropdown-menu:   0
Nav links total: 451
// All 451 links managed by ShowTab() JS tabs
// BreadcrumbList schema IS present and correct:
Home > Trophies > Baseball Trophies ✓
AFTER — Full mega menu with sport sub-links (this page is the reference)
Mega menu structure (from trophies-listing.html): 
<div class="mega" id="mega-trophies">
  <div class="mega-inner">  <!-- 190px + 1fr grid -->
    <div class="mp">
      <h2>Trophies</h2>
      <a href="/products/trophies"
         class="mega-browse">Browse All</a>
    </div>
    <div class="mc">
      <!-- sport sub-link grid -->
      <a href="/products/trophies?sport=afl">AFL</a>
      <a href="/products/trophies?sport=soccer">Soccer</a>
      <!-- 60+ sport links -->
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
// Back-port this entire block into index.html

Page Hero & H1

Hero Banner + Keyword-Optimised H1

The existing listing has no hero — the page opens immediately with sidebar + product grid. H1 "Baseball Trophies" is inside the left sidebar on a sub-category canonical URL. The revamp adds a full-width dark navy hero above the grid with H1 targeting the root /products/trophies page.

BEFORE — No hero, H1 in sidebar, sub-category canonical
Existing title, canonical and H1 (confirmed): 
<title>
  Shop Baseball Trophies Online | Free engraving
</title>

<link rel="canonical"
  href="https://alphatrophies.com.au/products/trophies/b/baseball"
>

<!-- H1 inside left sidebar, not in hero -->
<h1>Baseball Trophies</h1>

<title>Buy Trophies Online Australia
  | 2,400+ Personalised Trophies – Alpha Trophies
</title>

<link rel="canonical"
  href="https://alphatrophies.com.au/products/trophies"
>

<h1>Buy
  <span style="color:var(--gold)">Trophies</span>
  Online in Australia</h1>

<!-- Stat strip inside navy hero -->
<span>2,400+</span> products ·

Product Grid

Grid Layout & Image Alt Text

32 onclick GA4 tracking events on product card links — NOT a crawlability issue since href is present. However all 16 product images have empty alt="" which is a systematic template problem. The revamp applies the correct alt and title rule to all listing product images.

BEFORE — GA4 onclick on all cards, empty alt on all product images
GA4 onclick on all product card links (confirmed): 
// Not a crawl issue — href IS present
// But ALL product images have empty alt="":

<a href="/product/angel-victory-baseball"
   onclick="cacheProductsPos();(function(el){
     var d=JSON.parse(el.dataset.ga4Select);
     gtag('event','select_item',...);
   })(this)">
  <img src="..." alt="">
  <!-- empty alt on ALL 16 product images -->
</a>

// Python audit: 25 missing/empty alt on listing page
// Fix: apply alt rule in listing image template
ItemList schema with 16 products + prices ✓ present
AFTER — 3-col grid, filter sidebar, alt rule applied
Revamped — alt rule applied to all product cards: 
<a class="pc"
   href="/product/ballerina-figure-female-trophy">
  <img
    alt="Explore Ballerina Figure Female Trophy
         starting from $7.35"
    title="Buy Best Ballerina Trophies Online
           from Alpha Trophies from $7.35"
  >
  <div class="pc-name">Ballerina Figure Female Trophy</div>
  <div class="pc-price">from $7.35 <small>incl. GST</small></div>
</a>

// Alt: "Explore [Product] starting from [Price]"
// Title: "Buy Best [Product] Online from Alpha Trophies from [Price]"
Filter sidebar added: sport checkboxes, price range, height, material.

Below-Fold Content

The revamp adds a 4-type schema stack and replaces the sub-category copy with a structured content hub — trophy type comparison table, 4-step order flow, 10-Q FAQ and Google reviews section.

]

Page 3 of 3 — Product Detail
Page 3 of 3

Product Detail — /product/[slug]

The existing PDP has two H1 tags: the product name (correct) and "FREE LOGO CENTRE" (must be demoted — a one-line CMS template fix that applies across all PDPs). The title has a trailing pipe ("Argo Holder 50mm Insert Trophy 120MM |") because the site_name template variable is not resolving. Only BreadcrumbList schema is present — zero Product, Offer, AggregateRating, or FAQPage across the entire product catalogue. 221 images per PDP with 26 missing alt — a template-level fix. No related products section means all PageRank dead-ends at each PDP. The revamp adds 8 cross-sell links, a 5-Q product FAQ, full Product schema, size selector and live engraving preview.

H1 tags on page
(need exactly 1)
1
Schema types
(BreadcrumbList only)
0
Product/Offer/Rating
schema missing
221
images · 26 missing alt
per PDP
SEO

Product Ranking & Internal Equity

  • Demoting "FREE LOGO CENTRE" from H1 → H3 is a CMS template fix — applies across the entire product catalogue at once
  • Product/Offer/AggregateRating JSON-LD → price + stars + breadcrumb in SERP → estimated 15-30% CTR uplift on every product page across thousands of PDPs
  • 8 cross-sell links per PDP × thousands of products = systematic internal link equity distribution — currently 100% of PageRank dead-ends at each PDP
  • Title tag fix (trailing pipe → "| Alpha Trophies") → brand suffix visible in SERP → brand recognition and CTR uplift across the entire catalogue
  • Alt text template fix (26 per PDP) → image search equity for all product photography across the catalogue
AEO

Product-Level Answer Eligibility

  • 5-Q product FAQPage JSON-LD → per-product FAQ rich snippets → "what sizes does the Ballerina trophy come in?" answered directly in SERP
  • Specifications visible in DOM (not collapsed): Height / Material / Colour / Engraving / Availability — bots extract as product attribute structured data
  • Product Offer schema (price: 7.35, AUD, InStock) → Google Shopping integration, price in SERP, availability status
  • "Free laser engraving · No minimum order · GST included" in trust row → specific claims AI Overviews cite verbatim in engraving queries
  • AggregateRating in Product schema (4.9/169) → star snippet in SERP for every product URL → highest-impact schema fix on the site
GEO

LLM Product Citation

  • Specific product title ("Ballerina Figure Female On Marble Base Trophy 180MM") → LLMs extract and cite product names precisely in comparison and recommendation responses
  • Spec data (Antique gold / Laser engraved black on brushed gold plate / Ships 1-2 days) → LLMs cite these as verifiable product attributes
  • Price anchor ($7.35 incl. GST, free engraving) → LLMs include price when recommending products for "cheapest trophies Australia" prompts
  • Size selector consolidates variant URLs onto one canonical — reduces duplicate content that confuses LLM crawlers indexing product ranges
  • llm.txt footer link on every PDP → crawl-permission signal for AI indexers across the entire product catalogue
1
Navigation & Title

Title Tag Bug + Nav Consistency

Title ends with a trailing pipe on every PDP — a CMS template variable (site_name or equivalent) is not resolving. This is a one-line template fix. The nav is the same flat teal bar as other pages. BreadcrumbList schema is correctly implemented.

BEFORE — Trailing pipe in title, teal nav, BreadcrumbList only
Title bug confirmed (Python audit): 
<title>
  Argo Holder 50mm Insert Trophy 120MM |
</title>
<!-- site_name variable not resolving — blank after pipe
     Affects every PDP across the entire catalogue -->

// Fix in CMS product template:
<title>
  <?php echo get_the_title(); ?> | Alpha Trophies
</title>
// Or verify site_name variable in CMS settings globally
BreadcrumbList ✓ (Home > Trophies > Baseball Trophies > Product)
AFTER — Complete title, dark navy nav, breadcrumb in DOM
Revamped — complete title, breadcrumb in DOM: 
<title>Ballerina Figure Female On Marble Base
  Trophy 180MM | Alpha Trophies</title>

<meta name="description"
  content="Buy Ballerina Figure Female Trophy 180MM
  from Alpha Trophies. From $7.35 incl. GST, free
  laser engraving, ships 1-2 business days."
>

<!-- Visible breadcrumb in DOM + JSON-LD -->
Home / Trophies / Dance Trophies / Ballerina
2
Product Title & H1

Dual H1 — CMS Template Level Fix

Two H1 elements confirmed in the existing product-detail.html: "Argo Holder 50mm Insert Trophy 120MM" (correct) and "FREE LOGO CENTRE" (section header tagged as H1 — must be H3 or div). This is a one-line fix in the CMS product template that applies across all PDPs. Additionally, zero Product/Offer/AggregateRating schema on any PDP in the current site.

BEFORE — Dual H1 confirmed, collapsed description, zero product schema
Dual H1 confirmed (Python audit): 
// pd_h1s result:
[
  'Argo Holder 50mm Insert Trophy 120MM',  ✓
  'FREE LOGO CENTRE'                       ✗
]

// Fix — one-line in CMS product template:
// BEFORE:
<h1>FREE LOGO CENTRE</h1>
// AFTER:
<h3 class="section-heading">FREE LOGO CENTRE</h3>
Schema gap confirmed: 
Product schema:  NO   AggregateRating: NO
Offer schema:    NO   FAQPage:         NO
Only present:    BreadcrumbList ✓
Description/Specification is a collapsed accordion by default.
AFTER — Single H1, size tabs, live preview, full Product schema
Revamped — single H1, full Product schema: 
{
  "@type": "Product",
  "name": "Ballerina Figure Female On Marble Base Trophy 180MM",
  "sku": "EVT-M55208K+4598R",
  "offers": {
    "@type": "Offer",
    "price": "7.35",
    "priceCurrency": "AUD",
    "availability": "schema:InStock"
  },
  "aggregateRating": {
    "@type": "AggregateRating",
    "ratingValue": "4.9", "reviewCount": "169"
  }
}
// + BreadcrumbList + FAQPage (5 product Q&As)
// = price + stars + breadcrumb + FAQ in SERP
Size selector (180/210/250/350MM) + live engraving preview added.
3
Cross-Sell & Related Products

Zero Internal Links from PDPs → 8-product cross-sell

No related products section confirmed in code. Every PDP currently has zero internal links pointing to other product pages — 100% of PageRank dead-ends. 221 images per PDP with 26 missing alt is a systematic template issue. Description accordion is collapsed by default. The revamp adds 8 cross-sell product links, a 5-Q FAQ and visible specifications per PDP.

BEFORE — No cross-sell, no FAQ, accordion collapsed, 26 missing alt
Cross-sell confirmed missing (Python audit): 
// Audit result on existing-product-detail.html:
Related products: NOT FOUND
Cross-sell:       NOT FOUND

// Images per PDP — systematic template issue:
Total images on PDP:  221
Missing/empty alt:    26

// These 26 are nav sidebar + footer thumbnails
// rendered by same CMS template on every PDP
// Fix the template once — applies site-wide
Description accordion is collapsed by default. Specifications hidden from Google until JS expands.
AFTER — 8 related products, 5-Q FAQ, specs visible by default
Revamped — 8-product cross-sell per PDP: 
<!-- "You might also like" — 8 real <a href> links -->
<a class="rl-item"
   href="/product/badminton-shuttlecock-trophy">
  <img
    alt="Explore Badminton Shuttlecock Trophy starting from $7.05"
  >
  <div class="rl-name">Badminton Shuttlecock Trophy</div>
  <div class="rl-price">from $7.05</div>
</a>
// 8 of these per PDP = 8 new internal links per page
// Systematic equity distribution across catalogue
Specifications visible by default (not collapsed).
5-Q product FAQ with FAQPage JSON-LD per PDP.
Deployment
Deployment

Sign-off & Deployment Checklist

SEO Manager sign-off required before go-liveAll three pages require sign-off by Girish Kumar G before deployment. Schema must be validated in Google Rich Results Test.
